items.title: Pro-Net widens EV retail network amid strong e.Mas demand
items.summary: PRO-Net, the sales and distribution arm for Proton Holdings Bhd's new energy vehicle business, is expanding its retail network following strong sales growth for the Proton e.Mas brand in the first four months of 2026. As part of the expansion, Pro-Net has opened two new Proton e.Mas outlets this month. The new outlets also introduce Proton e.Mas' updated corporate identity, featuring redesigned showrooms with digital displays and upgraded customer service areas.

items.title: Tengku Amir Shah confers Selangor medals on 151 recipients in conjunction with Sultan’s 80th birthday
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 22 — The Raja Muda of Selangor, Tengku Amir Shah Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, yesterday conferred Selangor medals on 151 recipients in conjunction with the 80th birthday celebration of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. At the ceremony, one recipient was awarded the Pingat Kecemerlangan Sukan Selangor (PKS), while 21 individuals received Pingat Pekerti Terpilih (PPT) and 26 others were conferred the Pingat Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Selangor (PPC) medals. In addition, 87 individuals received Pingat Jasa Kebaktian (PJK), while 16 recipients were awarded Pingat Perkhidmatan Selangor (PPS) medals.

items.title: Police arrest man over viral schoolgirl molest allegations
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have arrested a 28-year-old man following a viral social media post alleging the molest of a schoolgirl in Seri Alam. Acting Johor police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat said police detected the viral post uploaded on Facebook by the account "Mohd Fadli Salleh" yesterday. Hoo said acting on information, a team from the Seri Alam district police headquarters Criminal Investigation Department arrested the suspect at 10.30pm yesterday.

items.title: Firefighters battle darkness, steep terrain and dead torch batteries to rescue five stranded surveyors in Kota Belud
items.summary: The Sabah Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Centre received an emergency call at 4.48pm on Wednesday, prompting eight personnel from Kota Belud Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) to the scene, located approximately 35km away. The operation involved a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) with four personnel, an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicle with two personnel and a four-wheel-drive Toyota Vigo with two personnel. He said the situation also hampered efforts to deliver food supplies to residents.

items.title: Dayang upgrade to 'Buy' with higher target price after Q1 results beat expectations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has upgraded Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd to "Buy" from "Hold", citing improving upstream activity, stronger vessel utilisation and potential contract wins that could drive earnings growth. Dayang's first-quarter core net profit of RM23 million exceeded CIMB Securities' expectations, accounting for 14 per cent of its previous full-year forecast, while broadly matching consensus estimates at 11.7 per cent. CIMB Securities said the company's vessel utilisation rate rose to 45 per cent in the first quarter (Q1) from 26 per cent a year earlier, helping narrow losses in its marine charter segment.

items.title: 'Bad timing': Trump says Iran war could keep him from son's wedding
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he would try to make it to his eldest son's wedding but that it was "bad timing" because of the Iran war. Donald Trump Jr, 48, is set to tie the knot for the second time this weekend, marrying Bettina Anderson in the Bahamas, US media reported. It's going to be just a small little private affair and I'm going to try and make it," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office when asked if he would attend his son's wedding.

items.title: Perak MACC remands three over alleged false medical claims worth RM53,000
items.summary: IPOH, May 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in Perak has obtained a remand order against three individuals — a government department director, an executive officer and a company owner — over the submission of documents containing false particulars involving health treatment claims amounting to about RM53,000. A source said a seven-day remand order until May 26 was issued against two suspects, a married couple in their 40s, by Magistrate A Hafiizh Abu Bakar after an application by the MACC at the Ipoh Magistrate’s Court on Wednesday morning. “A five-day remand order until May 24 was also issued against another male suspect, a government department director in his 50s.

items.title: Three dead after car crashes into cement lorry on Karak Highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three men were killed while a woman was seriously injured after the car they were travelling in crashed into the rear of a cement lorry at Km34 of the Kuala Lumpur-bound Karak Highway near Kampung Sungai Chinchin early today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department said the crash, involving a Volkswagen car and a cement lorry, was reported at 2.07am. "Firefighters from the Selayang fire station arrived at the scene at 2.35am.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against greenback, major currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened firmer against the US dollar and major currencies on Friday, supported by easing geopolitical tensions in Middle East and Malaysia's improving macroeconomic outlook. He said West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude prices fell 1.49 per cent and 2.32 per cent to US$97.79 per barrel and US$102.58 per barrel, respectively. The ringgit also traded higher against regional currencies.

items.title: SNCC says Malaysian Gaza flotilla activist Haroqs receives alleged Mossad-linked threats
items.summary: SEPANG, May 22 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has confirmed that Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activist Muhammad Hareez Adzrami, also known as Haroqs, has received threats believed to be linked to a proxy of the Israeli Zionist regime. In this regard, SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said that a number of security measures have been put in place for Haroqs, 26, and his mother. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had assured that the safety of Haroqs and his family members would be safeguarded.

items.title: GSF 2.0 mission ‘succeeded in shaking Israel’, says Sumud Nusantara chief
items.summary: SEPANG, May 22 — The Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission has been described as a success as it exerted major pressure on Israel and opened the world’s eyes to the regime’s cruelty towards humanitarian activists participating in the mission. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the mounting global pressure had also shaken Israel to the extent that efforts emerged to dissolve the Knesset following the circulation of a video allegedly showing the harsh treatment of GSF 2.0 humanitarian activists. Sani Araby said anger towards Israel had escalated to the point where the Zionist regime was receiving diplomatic demarches and diplomatic notes that no longer merely condemned but threatened over its harsh treatment of the activists.

items.title: Malaysians detained in flotilla mission arrive safely in Istanbul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: All 29 Malaysians detained by Israel in international waters while participating in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 humanitarian mission have arrived safely in Istanbul, Turkiye, at 5.45pm local time yesterday. Sumud Nusantara Command Center (SNCC) director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby was reported as saying that SNCC had been informed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim that Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan had dispatched three Turkish Airlines aircraft to Ramon Airport. SNCC expects the Malaysian delegation to depart for home tomorrow and arrive at Kuala Lumpur International Airport on Sunday.

items.title: Form Six student killed in three-vehicle collision
items.summary: In the 6pm incident, the victim, a student at Kolej Tingkatan Enam Tunku Abdul Rahman Putra, died at the scene. District police chief Superintendent Md Yusof Ahmad said the accident involved two motorcycles and a van. Yusof said the motorcyclist fell and sustained severe head injuries before being pronounced dead.

items.title: Trump 'very disappointed' with Nato allies over Iran war stance, Rubio says
items.summary: HELSINGBORG, Sweden: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet Nato allies on Friday for the first time since President Donald Trump called the alliance into question due to divisions over the Iran war and Washington announced plans to pull 5,000 troops from Europe. Before leaving for a meeting of Nato foreign ministers in Sweden, Rubio said Trump was "very disappointed" in alliance members who had not allowed the US to use bases on their territory for the war, singling out Spain in particular. "You have countries like Spain denying us the use of these bases - well then why are you in Nato?

items.title: Police: Nearly two million vehicles expected to flood Penang during long May 27 to June 2 break
items.summary: Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said this will prompt the state contingent of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to ramp up traffic monitoring and enforcement. To ensure smooth traffic flow and optimal road safety, PDRM, through the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT), will step up monitoring and enforcement operations at key locations,” he said in a statement last night. “We are also focusing on districts with the highest number of fatal accidents and congestion hotspots, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar–Jalan Bagan Luar in Seberang Perai Utara, Jalan Perusahaan Perai–Jalan Kulim in Seberang Perai Tengah, Jalan Besar Simpang Empat in Seberang Perai Selatan, and the Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway,” he said.

items.title: Stressful interrogations, isolation directly contributed to Zara Qairina’s alleged suicide, expert testifies
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 22 — The Coroner’s Court here yesterday heard that stressful interrogations by senior students and the subsequent isolation of Zara Qairina Mahathir had directly contributed to her alleged suicide. “I would like to highlight that the perceived child-parent conflict is not a direct factor in this suicide. “The direct factor here, in my opinion, is the stressful interrogations and the isolation that night or the following morning,” said the 67th witness before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: Genting Malaysia's Q1 earnings miss expectations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Malaysia Bhd's core earnings for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, fell sharply to RM10 million, down 74 per cent quarter-on-quarter and 81 per cent year-on-year, missing market expectations. Affin Hwang Investment Bank Bhd said the results accounted for only two per cent of its full-year forecast and one per cent of consensus estimates. International visitor numbers still rose four per cent year-on-year.

items.title: Selangor Sultan calls for continued focus on rakyat’s well-being amid global crisis, says Amirudin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has called on the Selangor government to continue prioritising the well-being of the people. Amirudin said he also presented updates on the Selangor Resilience Enhancement Package, which was formulated to assist the people and business sector affected by the crisis in West Asia. “May His Royal Highness the Sultan of Selangor and Her Royal Highness the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor continue to be blessed with good health, wisdom, guidance and strength, and remain enthroned in sovereignty and dignity over the state of Selangor,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: 9 to be charged over Bukit Bintang publicity stunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nine people are expected to be charged in court today following a viral publicity stunt involving flares and reckless driving to promote a clothing store in Bukit Bintang. Police also seized a pickup truck, two electric scrambler motorcycles and clothing worn during the incident. Investigations were carried out under Section 290 of the Penal Code for public nuisance, Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act for indecent behaviour, Section 6 of the Explosives Act 1957, Section 42 of the Road Transport Act for reckless and dangerous driving, and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act.

items.title: Heading to the Malaysia Cup final? Bukit Jalil LRT will run later than usual
items.summary: Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd (Rapid Rail) said in a statement yesterday that train operating hours for both the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line and the Putrajaya Line would also be extended to facilitate passenger movement after the match concludes. Only Sungai Besi Station will remain open for passengers transferring from the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line to the Putrajaya Line, the statement said. Rapid Rail also advised spectators to plan their journeys in advance and encouraged them to use Park N’ Ride facilities at selected stations along the Ampang/Sri Petaling and Putrajaya lines.

items.title: Two detained after helium cylinder explosion kills trader in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, May 22 — Two men in their 20s and 40s have been detained to assist investigations into a helium gas cylinder explosion at an expo site, which claimed the life of a trader near a supermarket here yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said investigations are still in the early stages to determine the cause of the incident and the police are also working to identify the owner of the helium gas cylinder. He said the site will be temporarily closed to allow investigations to be carried out.

items.title: Goldman Sachs to pay US$500m to settle shareholder lawsuit over 1MDB scandal
items.summary: NEW YORK, May 22 — Goldman Sachs has agreed to pay US$500 million (RM1.98 billion) to settle a class action lawsuit accusing the Wall Street bank of defrauding shareholders about its work for 1MDB, a Malaysian sovereign wealth fund that became embroiled in a corruption scandal. US and Malaysian authorities have said US$4.5 billion was siphoned away from 1MDB, with some diverted to offshore bank accounts and shell companies linked to Low. Goldman helped 1MDB sell US$6.5 billion of bonds and collected an estimated US$600 million in fees.

items.title: Texas sues Meta, WhatsApp over encryption privacy claims
items.summary: NEW YORK: The Texas Attorney General's Office on Thursday sued WhatsApp and its parent company Meta Platforms, alleging they misled consumers about the strength and scope of WhatsApp's encryption measures, which a Meta spokesman denied. The lawsuit filed in Harrison County court said WhatsApp and Meta falsely assured users that WhatsApp messages are encrypted despite having access to "virtually all" private communications on the messaging app. The lawsuit seeks a court order barring Meta and WhatsApp from accessing Texans' WhatsApp messages without their consent, as well as monetary penalties.

items.title: Wall Street banks boost Warner Bros loan to over US$10bil ahead of Paramount merger
items.summary: NEW YORK: Wall Street banks led by JPMorgan have increased the size of a loan package for Warner Bros Discovery to more than US$10 billion as the media company seeks to refinance debt ahead of its planned merger with Paramount Skydance, according to terms seen by Reuters on Thursday. The US dollar term loan was increased to US$9 billion from US$5 billion, while a one billion euro (US$1.16 billion) loan remained unchanged, the terms showed. The financing comes as Paramount moves ahead with its planned acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery, a deal valued at about US$110 billion that was signed recently after Netflix declined to raise its offer.

items.title: US stocks end volatile session higher as oil prices retreat
items.summary: NEW YORK: Wall Street stocks finished mostly higher on Thursday following a choppy session as markets tried to parse the prospects for a Middle East peace deal while digesting earnings from Nvidia and Walmart. After oil prices ascended for the first part of the day, benchmark futures contracts reversed course and finished down about two per cent. Major US indices finished modestly higher after starting in the red, with the broad-based S&P 500 ending up 0.2 per cent.

items.title: New York City offering US$50 World Cup tickets for residents
items.summary: NEW YORK: New York City will offer its residents a chance to snag US$50 tickets to World Cup games in New Jersey this summer, Mayor Zohran Mamdani said, with 1,000 affordable tickets up for grabs through a lottery system. The discounted tickets come with free round-trip bus transportation to the stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey and will be split across five group-stage and two knockout games, with 150 tickets available per match. According to TicketData, a ticket price tracking platform, the average "get-in" cost for a group-stage match in New York as of Wednesday was US$864.

items.title: Inmates thankful for second chance
items.summary: TAPAH: A prison inmate released under the Madani Yakin Licensed Prisoner Release (PBSL) programme described the opportunity as a blessing and a second chance. Hanum (not her real name), 25, from Seberang Perak, said returning to society through a job placement at a restaurant was a chance to prove that past mistakes would not be repeated. Prisons Department deputy commissioner-general (Rehabilitation) Hafidz Othman launched the programme.

items.title: 'Senior citizens bill to be tabled soon'
items.summary: THE government aims to table the Senior Citizens Bill in the next Parliament session. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said stakeholders were being consulted before it is finalised. "The bill is expected to include provisions on parental care responsibilities and the welfare of senior citizens to address the ageing population.

items.title: Zeti calls for talent overhaul to secure nation's growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Bank Negara governor Tan Sri Dr Zeti Akhtar Aziz said Malaysia's competitiveness hinges on its ability to overhaul talent development for a volatile global economy. Speaking at the FLUX Business Forum, Zeti said talent management is vital in building long-term economic resilience, especially as businesses face growing uncertainty from geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions and technological change. She added that a more skilled workforce would help Malaysia improve productivity, create new opportunities and strengthen the country's competitiveness.

items.title: WHO: Ebola outbreak to grow
items.summary: THE Ebola outbreak linked to more than 130 deaths in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo likely started two months ago and is expected to continue to grow, the World Health Organisation said on Wednesday. "Investigations are ongoing to ascertain when and where exactly this outbreak started," Anais Legand, WHO technical officer for viral threats, said. Six hundred suspected cases and 139 suspected deaths have been recorded so far, WHO director-general Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said here.

items.title: #NSTviral: Kajang Prison's famous 'karipap pusing' draws crowds - and jokes online
items.summary: KAJANG: Did you know that you can buy karipap from Kajang Prison? "You can simply walk into Kajang Prison and inform personnel at the guard post that you wish to go to Prismart Kajang Prison for the mutton curry puffs, or visit the My Pride Sales Gallery to shop for batik fabric, woven songket and frozen food products made by prison inmates. Kajang Prison's famed "karipap pusing" are especially popular for their flaky spiral pastry and generous fillings.

items.title: Brain drain deepens [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's brain drain reflects deeper structural issues in its wage system, career mobility and economic model as professionals continue to seek better opportunities overseas amid limited high-value jobs at home, an economist said. The outflow of talent points to weaknesses in Malaysia's ability to create globally competitive industries, retain innovation and provide long-term career pathways for highly educated workers. Associate Professor Dr Nik Ahmad Sufian Burhan, head of social and development sciences at Universiti Putra Malaysia's Faculty of Human Ecology, said Malaysia has yet to create enough high-value and high-skilled jobs to retain talent.

items.title: Managing the trepidation of new projects at work
items.summary: Every new project at work arrives with a mixture of opportunity and uncertainty. Even experienced professionals can feel uneasy when stepping into unfamiliar territory. A new assignment often brings different expectations, new people, tight deadlines, and the possibility of failure.

items.title: Goldman Sachs to pay US$500mil to settle shareholder lawsuit over 1MDB scandal
items.summary: NEW YORK: Goldman Sachs has agreed to pay US$500 million to settle a class action lawsuit accusing the Wall Street bank of defrauding shareholders about its work for 1MDB, a Malaysian sovereign wealth fund embroiled in a corruption scandal. US and Malaysian authorities have said US$4.5 billion was siphoned away from 1MDB, with some diverted to offshore bank accounts and shell companies linked to Low. Goldman helped 1MDB sell US$6.5 billion of bonds and collected an estimated US$600 million in fees.

items.title: Two detained over deadly helium cylinder blast in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Two men in their 20s and 40s have been detained to assist investigations into a helium gas cylinder explosion at an expo site, which claimed the life of a trader near a supermarket here yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said investigations are still in the early stages to determine the cause of the incident, and police are working to identify the owner of the cylinder. He said the site will be temporarily closed to allow investigations to proceed.

items.title: Gaza healthcare, aid systems 'decimated', NGOs warn at UN
items.summary: UNITED NATIONS: More than six months after the United Nations endorsed a peace plan in Gaza, the humanitarian situation there remains catastrophic, three global NGOs said Thursday, calling on Israel to respect its obligations. Major gaps remain between Israel's commitments and the reality facing Palestinians, representatives for Oxfam, Save the Children and Refugees International told reporters at UN headquarters in New York. "Israel continues to deny most experienced aid groups from bringing in essential supplies, like pipes to fix water systems, shelters, materials and medical supplies at the levels needed," Oxfam America President Abby Maxman said.

items.title: US tightens border checks for World Cup [WATCH]
items.summary: Diplomatic Security Service (DSS) regional security officer Jeffrey Kraus said the agency was working closely with consular officers, investigators and overseas missions to identify fraudulent travel documents and questionable visa applications. "There is obviously a risk of visa fraud. Kraus said passport and visa fraud was often linked to broader transnational criminal networks.

items.title: Coming soon: KL’s trains, buses to allow credit cards for tap-in, tap-out; Prasarana says project in award stage
items.summary: The other good news is that you will also be able to use other cashless payment options — such as debit cards, QR codes, mobile wallets (e.g. Current options for KL’s rail services: Buy a token or use TNG card Public transport operator Prasarana Malaysia Berhad explained why passengers currently cannot use credit cards, mobile wallets and QR codes yet to directly tap in and out for Rapid KL’s train services. Currently, you can use cash, credit cards or debit cards at a ticket machine at Rapid KL’s train stations... but only to buy a token or reload your Touch ‘n Go card.

items.title: First time using Kuala Lumpur’s trains? Here are some tips (including how to pay, tap in and out)
items.summary: Touch ‘n Go card: This is a stored-value card that you can tap in and out at fare gates, and can keep reusing by topping up the value in the card. Conclusion: For now, you can only board the train or BRT if you use a token or Touch ‘n Go card. Will you be using a token or a Touch 'n Go card to ride the trains in Kuala Lumpur?

items.title: ‘Extinct’ tiger milk mushroom returns to Perak forest through Orang Asli-led conservation project
items.summary: LENGGONG, May 22 — A conservation project involving the tiger milk mushroom, scientifically known as Lignosus rhinocerus, at the Piah Forest Reserve by Yayasan Bambusa has enabled the reintroduction of the species into its native wild forest habitat. Yayasan Bambusa founder and chief executive officer Dr Muhammad Hakim Abdullah said the project covers about 100 hectares in Compartment 194 of the forest reserve, which has been identified as a propagation site for the tiger milk mushroom, purported to be one of the few areas where the species is being bred in its natural forest habitat. Muhammad Hakim said the project has received full backing from the Perak state government, adding that Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad planted the first tiger milk mushroom seedling at the site in September 2024.

items.title: Malaysia urged to tighten maritime waste controls as traffic through Strait of Melaka grows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The head of a waste management consultancy has warned that Malaysia must impose a stricter maritime waste management system to protect the Strait of Melaka from environmental pollution, as shipping traffic is set to increase significantly through the passageway. This is in view that the incidence of oil spills can increase as more vessels ply between Europe and Asia and are redirected through the strait as an alternative route to avoid the conflict-stricken Strait of Hormuz, said Nur Zulaikha Yusof, head of sustainability and strategic planning at Hexagon Synergy Group. She also said there should be coordination between Malaysia, Singapore and Indonesia, which share the Strait of Melaka waterway, to ensure effective enforcement.

items.title: US voices hope for Iran deal progress as Pakistan army chief heads to Teheran
items.summary: TEHERAN: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday voiced hope of progress on ending the war with Iran, with mediator Pakistan's army chief poised for talks in Teheran. The role played by Field Marshal Asim Munir, a powerful figure with a growing role in Pakistan's foreign relations, has taken on increased significance as US President Donald Trump warned that negotiations to end the war were on the "borderline" between a deal and renewed strikes. On Thursday, Iran's ISNA news agency said Munir's visit was aimed at continuing "talks and consultations" with Iranian authorities, without providing details.

items.title: 'They treat their animals better': Gaza flotilla activist alleges abuse by Israeli forces
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Julien Cabral has just completed his first voyage with the "Flotilla for Gaza." "They first jammed communications then boarded in broad daylight with guns and fired plastic bullets just for fun," said Cabral. Cabral said the Israelis were marine commandos.

items.title: Trump sending 5,000 US troops to Poland
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he was sending 5,000 troops to Poland, after Washington said an earlier planned deployment had been called off amid pressure on Europe to fend for itself. Trump said the move was based on his relationship with Poland's president Karol Nawrocki, a nationalist ally whom he said he was "proud to endorse" in elections that Nawrocki won last year. "I am pleased to announce that the United States will be sending an additional 5,000 Troops to Poland," Trump said on his Truth Social network.

items.title: AI angst rising among tech-savvy Gen Z
items.summary: THE AI revolution is here and the boos are getting louder. "It will touch every profession, every classroom, every hospital, every laboratory, every person, and every relationship you have," he said as boos rang out even as he addressed anxieties about job security and an uncertain future. Many tech firms are also cutting staff, citing AI.

items.title: Israel releases detained flotilla activists, 29 Malaysians due back in Malaysia May 24
items.summary: SEPANG, May 21 — All 428 humanitarian activists aboard the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0, including 29 Malaysians detained by Israeli forces in international waters, have been released, with the Malaysian group due to return home May 24. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the SNCC had been informed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim that Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan had dispatched three Turkish Airlines aircraft to Ramon Airport, located in Israeli territory. He said the Malaysian activists were expected to fly home on May 23 (Saturday) and arrive at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) on May 24.

items.title: Tony Pua gives statement to police over Rukun Negara Facebook post, says he wrote it without duress
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Former Damansara MP Tony Pua today confirmed he has provided a witness statement to police over his Facebook post on the Rukun Negara. Pua said he was contacted by Shah Alam district police at about 1.30pm to arrange the appointment under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code. Pua said he was told the investigation followed more than 80 police reports lodged nationwide, with the first made in Sri Muda, Shah Alam.

items.title: Federal Highway motorcycle lane upgrade hits 43pc progress, Works Minister targets August completion
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — The RM20 million motorcycle lane upgrade project on the Federal Highway (FT002) has now reached 43 per cent and is expected to be fully completed by August. Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the high-impact project also includes the installation of lighting facilities worth RM6 million to illuminate the entire stretch for the safety of riders. He said this after inspecting several project sites through the Field Trip Programme to the Federal Highway Motorcycle Lane Upgrade Project in Batu Tiga, here today.

items.title: Bukit Aman probes alleged fake TikTok account insulting King, warns public on 3R issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has opened an investigation paper into defamatory and insulting content against His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, believed to have been posted via a fake TikTok account. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the investigation was being conducted by the Classified Criminal Investigation Unit (USJT). “Yes, we have opened an investigation paper and are conducting further investigations through USJT, Bukit Aman CID,” he said.

items.title: Police record Tony Pua’s statement over Facebook post on monarchy, Rukun Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Police recorded former Damansara MP Tony Pua’s statement today over his Facebook post that allegedly touched on sensitive issues involving the constitutional monarchy and Rukun Negara. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigations Department (CID) director Datuk M Kumar said the Selangor CID was still taking Tony Pua’s statement at the Selangor contingent police headquarters. The statement is believed to have been made following the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah’s, decree advising Seri Kembangan state assemblyman Wong Siew Ki and former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu to visit Dataran Selangor once a week to understand and appreciate the true meaning of the Rukun Negara.

items.title: MOT defends single‑sourcing RFP for Penang Mutiara Line LRT, cites legal and technical reasons
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — Every decision regarding the Penang Mutiara Line LRT project is made based on careful technical, legal and fiscal considerations to ensure that the people of the state have access to the public transport system. This project was originally a Penang State Government project under the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). To implement PTMP, MOT announced that the State Government appointed SRS Consortium Sdn Bhd as the Project Delivery Partner (PDP) through an open tender in August 2015 In March 2024, the Federal Government agreed to finance and fully take over the Mutiara Line LRT from the State Government on the condition that the reclamation project be limited to one island only, following Cabinet approval, with MRT Corp appointed as the developer and asset owner of the project.

items.title: Johor businessman claims loan sharks threatened children’s lives after alleged RM50,000 loan scam
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 21 — A 36-year-old businessman is now living in fear after several moneylenders threatened to kidnap and sell his son’s organs, in addition to injuring him and burning down his house over a loan application. Chan Chee Hau said he received the threat shortly after he applied to take out a RM50,000 loan meant for working capital for his car rental business in Kuala Lumpur. His ordeal started on May 8 after he saw a loan advertisement on Facebook that used a licensed loan company logo.

items.title: Two Ducati superbike riders charged after viral wheelie stunt along Setiawangsa‑Pantai Expressway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Two high-powered motorcycle riders pleaded not guilty in the Magistrates’ Court here today to charges of performing dangerous ‘wheelie’ stunts on the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) early this month. Khairul Azham Mohd Noor, 36, who works as a clerk, and Nashril Rosli, 31, a mechanic, made the plea after the charges against them were read out separately before Magistrate Tg Syazwany Yasmyn Tuan Roslan. Earlier, Prosecuting Officer Inspector Shazwan Hasifi Shahrun offered bail at RM2,000 for Nashril and RM3,000 for Khairul Azham, respectively, with one surety.

items.title: Children’s home caregiver gets 11 years behind bars, five strokes for abusing two young boys
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — A caregiver at a children’s welfare home was sentenced by the Ampang Sessions Court today to 11 years’ imprisonment and five strokes of the cane after pleading guilty to six charges involving sexual offences and unnatural sex against two boys earlier this year. The court imposed one stroke of the cane for each of the first five charges, while no whipping sentence was imposed for the offence under Section 15(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017. In addition, the judge ordered the accused to undergo counselling throughout his imprisonment and placed him under police supervision for two years upon completion of his sentence.

items.title: Police probe sudden death after body of man found in drain near George Town shophouses
items.summary: The New Straits Times reported that passersby alerted police at 9.26am after discovering the man unconscious in the drain. North-east district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said initial checks identified the deceased as a local man from Kapar, Selangor. He said police believed the man had been homeless and staying in the area where he was found.

items.title: Police arrest eight youths after teasing escalates into fight outside Klang secondary school
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Police arrested eight local men, including school students, believed to have been involved in a fight near a national secondary school in Klang on May 19. Klang Selatan District Police Chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said all the suspects, aged between 14 and 24, were detained by a police team from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Klang Selatan District Police Headquarters (IPD) immediately after receiving a report regarding the incident at 1.00am yesterday. “Preliminary investigations revealed that some of those arrested are still school students, while the rest are youths who are no longer in school,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out real-time dashboard to track global supply crisis impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The Ministry of Economy together with the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) has launched a new digital platform to monitor how the global supply crisis is affecting the country’s economy in near real time. The initiative, known as the Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard, is a fully integrated system designed to track economic spillovers from global disruptions, including energy prices, trade flows and supply chain pressures, according to a joint announcement by Economy Ministry and Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). The dashboard provides users with up-to-date economic indicators, including movements in the cost of living, fluctuations in commodity and global market prices, as well as analysis of Malaysia’s trade performance and broader economic trends.

items.title: Malaysia’s longest river bridge opens today as RM848.75m Batang Lupar link connects Sebuyau and Maludam
items.summary: 1 Bridge, linking Sebuyau and Maludam, which will open to traffic today, is the longest river-crossing bridge in Malaysia. The 1.7-kilometre-long Sungai Johor Bridge was previously the country’s longest river-crossing bridge. Spanning approximately 4.8 kilometres across the Batang Lupar, the new bridge forms part of Sarawak’s wider infrastructure transformation under the Coastal Road Network and Second Trunk Road Programme aimed at improving connectivity between coastal areas and major towns.

items.title: Trapped lorry driver found dead after premix‑laden truck overturns into muddy ditch in Sebuyau
items.summary: KUCHING, May 21 — A lorry driver was found dead after being trapped under mud following a self-accident involving a premix-laden lorry along Jalan Batang Lupar–Sadong near Kampung Tebeluk, Sebuyau today. According to a statement, police initial findings suggested the incident occurred around 5am after a front tyre burst, causing the driver to lose control before the lorry skidded into a muddy ditch. “The accident caused the driver to become trapped inside the lorry under a pile of mud,” the department said in a statement.

items.title: Selangor police bust four call centre syndicates, 43 suspects taken into custody
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Police arrested 43 individuals, including a local citizen, believed to be involved in four separate call centre syndicates following several raids around Shah Alam and Puchong on May 19 and yesterday. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said that through the arrests at a two-story bungalow in Setia Alam here on May 19, police detained 13 foreigners and a local citizen believed to be involved in a part-time job offer scam targeting Iraqi nationals. Meanwhile, Shazeli said police also arrested 29 foreigners, including two women, in three separate raids conducted between 11.00pm and 1.20am at Bandar 16 Sierra, Puchong, on May 19 and yesterday.
